channel.title: Today's WTF
channel.description: Critical analysis of technology, privacy, and the future.
channel.ns0:author: Giulio Gabrieli

item.title: Today's WTF - 2026-06-07
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Journey to JPEG XL: How open source experiments shaped the future of image coding: https://opensource.googleblog.com/2026/06/journey-to-jpeg-xl-how-open-source-experiments-shaped-the-future-of-image-coding.html?m=1
- Automating Obsidian Workflow for Large Academic Vaults - The Effortless Academic: https://effortlessacademic.com/automating-obsidian-workflows-for-large-academic-vaults/
- Meet Dreambeans, an app that connects you with what matters: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/dreambeans/
- Gemini for Science: AI experiments and tools for a new era of discovery: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/research/gemini-for-science-io-2026/
- New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/new-chatgpt-lockdown-mode-limits-tools.html
- My favorite AI coding tool isn't OpenCode: it's something way better (and cooler): https://www.xda-developers.com/my-favorite-ai-coding-tool-isnt-opencode-its-something-even-better/
- Local LLMs weren't enough, so I use a two-tier system that keeps my sensitive stuff offline: https://www.xda-developers.com/local-llm-falls-back-to-cloud-but-private-stuff-never-leaves-house/
- Not all ESP32 boards are built equal—here's why the manufacturer actually matters: https://www.howtogeek.com/not-all-esp32-boards-are-built-equal-why-manufacturer-matters/?shem=dsdf,sharefoc,agadiscoversdl,,sh/x/discover/m1/4
- I tried Google's new Antigravity 2.0, and I'm sticking with Claude Code: https://www.xda-developers.com/claude-codes-hooks-the-most-powerful-feature-nobody-talks-about-enough/

item.title: Today's WTF - 2026-06-08
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- 3 free, open-source apps to cancel your subscriptions this weekend (Jun 5–7): https://www.howtogeek.com/free-open-source-apps-to-cancel-your-subscriptions-this-weekend-jun-5-7/
- The future of the web is weirdly human: https://www.jonoalderson.com/conjecture/weirdly-human/
- Majorana 2, made more reliable with Microsoft Discovery agentic AI: https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/innovation/majorana-2-microsoft-discovery-agentic-ai/
- "Chat is dead." - Spyglass: https://spyglass.org/chatgpt-is-dead-long-live-chatgpt/
- How to Combine Claude Code and Codex for Maximum Coding Power: https://towardsdatascience.com/how-to-combining-claude-code-and-codex-for-max-coding-power/

item.title: Gemini on the couch, Proton on the run
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Proton Drive is Now Faster (And Getting a Linux Client Soon): https://itsfoss.com/news/proton-drive-encryption-upgrades/
- Built to benefit everyone: our plan: https://openai.com/index/built-to-benefit-everyone-our-plan/?utm_source=www.therundown.ai&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=apple-s-new-siri-ai-overhaul-is-here-sort-of
- The Gemini app becomes more agentic, delivering proactive, 24/7 help: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/products/gemini-app/next-evolution-gemini-app/
- Google's New Colab CLI Lets Developers and AI Agents Run Python on Remote Colab GPUs and TPUs From the Terminal: https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/06/googles-new-colab-cli-lets-developers-and-ai-agents-run-python-on-remote-colab-gpus-and-tpus-from-the-terminal/

item.title: Desktop Dreams, AI Nightmares
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- I turned my Android phone into a laptop replacement, and it's more productive than I expected: https://www.xda-developers.com/android-phone-laptop-replacement-more-productive-expected/?user=Z2Fjazk0QGdtYWlsLmNvbQ&lctg=f7eaffe8f05e51fe894998ad1f80581524527f5275ed618740b939af74e7cd25
- Microsoft built its most powerful Surface ever on Nvidia's new chip, and we saw every RTX Spark competitor coming this fall: https://www.xda-developers.com/microsoft-build-most-powerful-surface-nvidia-new-chip-every-rtx-spark-competitor/?user=Z2Fjazk0QGdtYWlsLmNvbQ&lctg=f7eaffe8f05e51fe894998ad1f80581524527f5275ed618740b939af74e7cd25
- Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5, Its Most Powerful AI Yet, With Cyber Safeguards: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/anthropic-releases-claude-fable-5-its.html?m=1

item.title: AI Cults and Ceiling Lights
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Target Encoding Divergence: A Machine Learning Engineering Perspective: https://flyback.ai/engineering/target-encoding-divergence
- North Mini Code: Agentic Coding Model for Developers | Cohere: https://cohere.com/blog/north-mini-code
- When AI Becomes a Religion: https://open.substack.com/pub/luizasnewsletter/p/when-ai-becomes-a-religion?utm_source=share&utm_medium=android&r=4z5lx
- Philips has just launched an impressive new ceiling light range that mimics the appearance of a skylight, and I think it looks incredible — but its lack of smart features might not make up for its hefty price tag: https://www.techradar.com/home/smart-lights/philips-just-launched-a-new-ceiling-light-that-looks-like-a-skylight-and-i-think-it-looks-incredible-but-its-lack-of-smart-features-makes-its-hefty-price-tag-hard-to-justify
- Google Gemini Smart Glasses Launching In Fall To Take On Meta: https://www.uploadvr.com/gemini-smart-glasses-are-launching-in-fall-to-take-on-ray-ban-meta/

item.title: Smart Glasses, Dumb Ideas
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- You can just say it: https://noperator.dev/posts/you-can-just-say-it/
- PMSG - Prototype Modular Smart Glasses - Hackster.io: https://www.hackster.io/psmooij/pmsg-prototype-modular-smart-glasses-8bd4e6
- Project Tapestry: The Path to Frontier Sovereign AI: https://thealliance.ai/blog/project-tapestry-the-path-to-frontier-sovereign-ai?utm_campaign=41922644-Tapestry&utm_medium=email&_hsmi=423360194&utm_content=423330123&utm_source=hs_email
- DiffusionGemma: 4x faster text generation: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/diffusion-gemma-faster-text-generation/

item.title: Anthropic’s Border Control Blues
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/us-orders-anthropic-to-suspend-fable-5.html?m=1
- Best Python AI Frameworks in 2026: https://blog.jetbrains.com/pycharm/2026/06/best-python-ai-frameworks-in-2026/
- IBM and Google Cloud Announce Strategic Partnership to Scale AI with Human Expertise and AI-Powered Delivery: https://www.googlecloudpresscorner.com/2026-06-04-IBM-and-Google-Cloud-Announce-Strategic-Partnership-to-Scale-AI-with-Human-Expertise-and-AI-Powered-Delivery?linkId=62237018

item.title: Worming Into Your Local AI
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- ThreatsDay Bulletin: Worm Code Leaked, AI Agent Phished, Claude Code Patch + 28 New Stories: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/threatsday-bulletin-worm-code-leaked-ai.html#fake-banking-updates
- Researchers Build Self-Replicating AI Worm That Operates Entirely on Local, Open-Weight Models: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/researchers-build-self-replicating-ai.html
- PlotStudio AI for Academic Data Analysis: In-Depth Review - The Effortless Academic: https://effortlessacademic.com/plotstudio-ai-for-academic-data-analysis-in-depth-review/

item.title: Bugs in Space and Code
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2026/06/13/nasa-humans-spend-longer-in-space/: https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2026/06/13/nasa-humans-spend-longer-in-space/
- An AI Agent Infiltrated Fedora's Bug Tracker and Wreaked Havoc: https://itsfoss.com/news/fedora-bug-tracker-infiltrated-by-ai-agent/

item.title: Promptly Deleted Your Intelligence
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Stop guessing which local AI models fit your hardware — this free tool does it for you: https://www.xda-developers.com/open-source-tool-is-the-first-step-before-trying-out-local-ai-llms-llmfit/?link_source=ta_first_comment&taid=6a2f2b6d533f230001228d88
- Your home network doesn't need Pi-hole — an $8 ESP32 blocks ads just as well for a fraction of the cost: https://www.xda-developers.com/your-home-network-doesnt-need-pi-hole-but-an-esp32/?link_source=ta_first_comment&taid=6a2dafbeca10da0001d977eb
- NotebookLM's Audio Overviews sound so human, you'll believe the misinformation: https://www.xda-developers.com/notebooklm-audio-overviews-sound-human-believe-misinformation/
- I borrowed Claude prompts from Anthropic engineers and immediately stopped wasting time on bad ones: https://www.xda-developers.com/i-borrowed-claude-prompts-from-anthropic-engineers/
- I replaced my entire AI prompt library with Fabric, and now I can't imagine going back: https://www.xda-developers.com/replaced-ai-prompt-library-fabric-cant-go-back/

item.title: Flipper Your Local AI
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=unpaidsoc&utm_campaign=fy26q2-googlecloudtech-blog-ai-in_feed-no-brand-global&utm_content=-&utm_term=-&linkId=62355608
- Flipper One is a Pocket-sized Linux Cyberdeck - It's FOSS: https://itsfoss.com/news/flipper-one-announcement/
- The UK's social media ban for children: the privacy problems Australia already exposed: https://proton.me/blog/uk-social-media-ban-privacy
- Running local models is good now: https://vickiboykis.com/2026/06/15/running-local-models-is-good-now/?utm_campaign=Data_Elixir&utm_source=Data_Elixir_577

- How the Open Knowledge Format can improve data sharing: https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/data-analytics/how-the-open-knowledge-format-can-improve-data-sharing?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=unpaidsoc&utm_campaign=fy26q2-googlecloudtech-blog-ai-in_feed-no-brand-global&utm_content=-&utm_term=-&linkId=62355608

item.title: Commodore's Flip Phone Flop
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Commodore's comeback now includes a flip phone that blocks social media: https://www.xda-developers.com/commodore-is-back-with-a-flip-phone-that-blocks-social-media/
- A used workstation costs less than a new GPU, but it pays for itself in ways gaming hardware never will: https://www.xda-developers.com/a-used-workstation-costs-less-than-a-new-gpu-but-it-pays-for-itself-in-ways-gaming-hardware-never-will/
- I Created a Modern Home Lab Blueprint. Here's What Made the Cut: https://www.virtualizationhowto.com/2026/06/i-created-a-modern-home-lab-blueprint-heres-what-made-the-cut/
- Bambu Lab Just Dropped a 'Pure' PLA: Here is What's Actually Inside It: https://all3dp.com/4/bambu-lab-just-dropped-a-pure-pla-here-is-whats-actually-inside-it/

item.title: Vibe Coding Your Vision Away
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Article Content Unavailable: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/17/g7-trump-ai-tech-leaders-openai-anthropic-google.html?utm_source=www.therundown.ai&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=inside-the-deadlock-keeping-mythos-offline
- Vibe Coding Bible: A Programming Paradigm for AI-Written Software: https://dev.to/evgeniy_kormin/vibe-coding-bible-a-programming-paradigm-for-ai-written-software-gp9?context=digest&ahoy_click=true&t=ZuSkr7RgrhMZ16xtjNV3pmHMl7tbAMfA&s=DYlHCGTOq_qYnhdfDjXlEdVMUdQ8jmwbDP6Bgi-_22U&u=https%253A%252F%252Fdev.to%252Fevgeniy_kormin%252Fvibe-coding-bible-a-programming-paradigm-for-ai-written-software-gp9%253Fcontext%253Ddigest
- Raven Prism is a Linux Computer That Happens To Be A Pair of Glasses: https://itsfoss.com/news/raven-prism-linux-smart-glass/
- A Guide to Andrej Karpathy's AutoResearch: Automating ML with AI Agents: https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/guide-to-autoresearch
- ChatGPT's market share slips below 50% for first time: https://www.google.com/url?q=https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%252F%252Ftechcrunch.com%252F2026%252F06%252F16%252Fchatgpts-market-share-slips-below-50-for-first-time%252F%253Futm_source%3Dtldrai/1/0100019edb00f2d0-56aabb1a-c900-4408-9a62-24425a23c04b-000000/BjrxCMX0q-XnSgz0ER9GeJJMeeImVCv7H7PCsYlZJRE%3D452&source=gmail&ust=1781877177028000&usg=AOvVaw3tz3CkBFw0IApxqP4Um9C1
- Linux Kernel 7.1 is a Feature Release That Could Be Useful For You: https://itsfoss.com/news/linux-kernel-7-1-release/?ref=foss-weekly-newsletter
- GitHub - AI4Scientist/nano-scientist: An autonomous research agent that turns a topic into a peer-reviewed technical report: https://github.com/AI4Scientist/nano-scientist

item.title: AI Hijack, Local Stack Attack
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution: https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/autojack-attack-lets-one-web-page.html
- 3 impressive ESP32 projects to make this weekend (Jun 19 - 21): https://www.howtogeek.com/impressive-esp32-projects-to-make-this-weekend-jun-19-21/
- Home Assistant OS 18: kernel, VM, firmware Raspberry e molto altro: https://indomus.it/news/home-assistant-os-18/
- 16GB 'Bonkers' Xeon PC is back at Newegg for Prime Day with $209 price tag, Nvidia Geforce GPU, 256GB SSD - Is this the best PC deal of 2026?: https://www.techradar.com/pro/16gb-bonkers-xeon-pc-is-back-at-newegg-for-prime-day-with-usd209-price-tag-nvidia-geforce-gpu-256gb-ssd-is-this-the-best-pc-deal-of-2026
- I replaced my entire browser extension stack with one local LLM, and I'm not going back: https://www.xda-developers.com/replaced-entire-browser-extension-stack-local-llm-not-going-back/

item.title: AI Has An Email Address
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- OpenAI prepares GPT-5.6 models for the upcoming release: https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-prepares-gpt-5-6-models-for-the-upcoming-release/?utm_source=tldrai
- Espanso is the text expander I didn't know I needed, and it's completely free: https://www.xda-developers.com/espanso-text-expander-didnt-know-needed-completely-free/?utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=XDA-202606200710&utm_source=XDA-NL&user=Z2Fjazk0QGdtYWlsLmNvbQ&lctg=f7eaffe8f05e51fe894998ad1f80581524527f5275ed618740b939af74e7cd25
- Atomic Mail lets AI agents sign up for their own email accounts | AIntelligenceHub: https://go.aintelligencehub.com/ma-atomicmailagents
- Perplexity Launches Brain, a Self-Improving Memory System That Builds a Context Graph of an Agent's Work and Learns Overnight: https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/06/18/perplexity-launches-brain/?utm_source=www.aidevsignals.com&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=ai-intelligence-brief-the-week-ai-memory-got-a-brain-and-agents-got-a-directory
- These low-power chips beat the ESP32 for battery life: https://www.howtogeek.com/low-power-chips-beat-the-esp32-for-battery-life/
- It seems a copyright law quirk will finally let Lord British reclaim Ultima from EA’s clutches: https://www.destructoid.com/it-seems-a-copyright-law-quirk-will-finally-let-lord-british-reclaim-ultima-from-eas-clutches/

item.title: Fox, Linux, and Open Source
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- https://share.google/XL6d4ZKP3egUzxzNj: https://share.google/XL6d4ZKP3egUzxzNj
- Love NotebookLM but hate Google? Try this open-source alternative: https://www.androidauthority.com/notebooklm-alternative-open-notebook-hands-on-3677932/
- Firefox Can Do All This? 21 Features Most Users Never Touch: https://itsfoss.com/firefox-additional-features/
- 5 pioneering Linux distros that quietly faded into history: https://www.howtogeek.com/these-pioneering-linux-distros-quietly-faded-into-history/

item.title: Windows Embraces Its Inner Nerd
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Windows is quietly becoming the easiest way to run Linux containers, and I don't know how to feel about it: https://www.xda-developers.com/windows-is-quietly-becoming-the-easiest-way-to-run-linux-containers/?user=Z2Fjazk0QGdtYWlsLmNvbQ&lctg=f7eaffe8f05e51fe894998ad1f80581524527f5275ed618740b939af74e7cd25
- What computers can't do (and what designers should): https://uxdesign.cc/what-computers-cant-do-and-what-designers-should-d9d55c4fe2a1
- I tested Google's new Gemma 4 12B on my 8GB GPU, and now I don't want to go back to smaller models: https://www.xda-developers.com/tested-google-gemma-4-12b-on-8gb-gpu-and-dont-want-to-go-back-to-smaller-models/?link_source=ta_first_comment&taid=6a34949d0b835600015e6eb9
- I tested 3 tiny local LLMs for everyday work, and only one of them impressed me: https://www.xda-developers.com/tested-tiny-local-llms-for-everyday-work-only-one-impressed-me/?link_source=ta_first_comment&taid=6a3834be0b83560001672829

item.title: Google’s Hallucination Trenchcoat
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Google AI Overviews State Some Fiction-Content as Facts - WinBuzzer: https://winbuzzer.com/2026/06/23/google-ai-overviews-may-present-scp-fiction-as-fact-xcxwbn/
- A coding agent is six functions in a trenchcoat: https://open.substack.com/pub/tidydesign/p/a-coding-agent-is-six-functions-in?utm_source=share&utm_medium=android&r=4z5lx
- https://elink56e.dataelixir.com/ss/c/u001.Dt8L-Y0jq6TkmYqlU8wlZHgJ3FmmRhkFu4Is0iMd6mQ94YdRiU4WjxczWIHts96dHlWUvDuPc6Ofwr7ymbx-kzy_xGI6dTAMyHLPUm4zlAle7jzpNCNeiTZoClkvsauKx9VZqKGsxyZIwVIzRiM2zvssVdi87XQyvSHDEI3NPKDHaZI5iEFcBuFy67M4ymtvuEbXtmSOLSneG9070avqzQ/4rq/SnVsqebuSWehRkX3cJgNGA/h11/h001.JJHQvpPsAkyzuDPHAhMFW1ZxdTtUeNY8JzusIE28ZsE: https://elink56e.dataelixir.com/ss/c/u001.Dt8L-Y0jq6TkmYqlU8wlZHgJ3FmmRhkFu4Is0iMd6mQ94YdRiU4WjxczWIHts96dHlWUvDuPc6Ofwr7ymbx-kzy_xGI6dTAMyHLPUm4zlAle7jzpNCNeiTZoClkvsauKx9VZqKGsxyZIwVIzRiM2zvssVdi87XQyvSHDEI3NPKDHaZI5iEFcBuFy67M4ymtvuEbXtmSOLSneG9070avqzQ/4rq/SnVsqebuSWehRkX3cJgNGA/h11/h001.JJHQvpPsAkyzuDPHAhMFW1ZxdTtUeNY8JzusIE28ZsE

item.title: RAMming Through Your AI Chips
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Home Assistant OS 18.0 is out, and it'll stop devouring your RAM on low-end devices: https://www.xda-developers.com/home-assistant-os-180-is-out-and-itll-stop-devouring-your-ram-on-low-end-devices/?link_source=ta_first_comment&taid=6a3a521e1554950001f28c9b
- How I use premortems with Claude and Codex: https://dev.to/pablonax/how-i-use-premortems-with-claude-and-codex-46mm
- OpenAI launches new security tools and updates GPT-5.5-Cyber: https://www.testingcatalog.com/openai-launches-new-security-tools-and-updates-gpt-5-5-cyber/
- https://openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip/: https://openai.com/index/openai-broadcom-jalapeno-inference-chip/

item.title: Open Source Your Speaker
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- PINE64's Smart Speaker is a Home Assistant Powered Alternative to Amazon Echo: https://itsfoss.com/news/pinevoice-smart-speaker/
- FOSS Weekly #26.26: Free Origin Access, Niri Tiling, Firefox Tricks, Myna in Ubuntu and More Linux Stuff: https://itsfoss.com/newsletter/foss-weekly-26-26/

item.title: Local AI Outsmarts Big Tech
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- Open Design is replacing ComfyUI for my local AI workflows, and the difference is massive: https://www.xda-developers.com/open-design-is-replacing-comfyui-for-local-ai-design-workflows/
- Louder ESP32 Mini board adds WiFi and Bluetooth to old speakers for Squeezelite, Snapclient, or ESPHome support: https://www.cnx-software.com/2026/06/25/louder-esp32-mini-board-adds-wifi-and-bluetooth-to-old-speakers-for-squeezelite-snapclient-or-esphome-support/
- I run a 24GB GPU instead of paying for Claude or Codex, and Qwen 3.6 keeps up more than I expected: https://www.xda-developers.com/24gb-gpu-instead-of-paying-for-claude-or-codex-qwen-36-is-impressive/
- 8 Best Gadget Gifts Every Man Actually Wants This 4th of July: https://www.yankodesign.com/2026/06/22/8-best-gadget-gifts-every-man-actually-wants-this-4th-of-july/

item.title: Pi, Print, Play, Repeat
item.description:
Links discussed in this episode:
- How to do Object Detection with Raspberry Pi Using CircuitDigest Cloud: https://circuitdigest.com/microcontroller-projects/raspberry-pi-object-detection-using-circuitdigest-cloud
- Your Raspberry Pi Doesn't Have to Sit in a Drawer—Here Are 7 Ways to Use Yours Every Day: https://www.howtogeek.com/your-raspberry-pi-doesnt-have-to-sit-in-a-drawerhere-are-ways-to-use-yours-every-day/
- 5 more ESP32-powered 3D printing projects to make this weekend (Jun 26 - 28): https://www.howtogeek.com/more-esp32-powered-3d-printing-projects-weekend-jun-26-28/
- 3 trending free, open source apps to replace your defaults this weekend (Jun 26-28): https://www.howtogeek.com/3-trending-free-open-source-apps-to-replace-your-defaults-this-weekend-jun-26-28/
